hero.title: Research
hero.subtitle: Advancing knowledge, driving innovation, and extending the University's impact through rigorous inquiry, meaningful discovery, and community-centered research.

overview.title: Overview
paragraphs: Research is the lifeblood of a university committed to truth — and at AUF, it is both a mission and a mandate. Through systematic inquiry, creative exploration, and collaborative discovery, AUF researchers contribute to the advancement of knowledge in ways that directly address the needs of local communities and the global academic community.

AUF's research ecosystem brings together faculty researchers, student scholars, research centers, and external partners across disciplines — generating outputs that are not only academically rigorous but socially relevant. From health sciences breakthroughs to educational innovations and engineering solutions, AUF research touches every domain of human concern.

Guided by the Office of the Vice President for Research and Innovation (OVPRI), the university maintains an environment where curiosity is nurtured, excellence is rewarded, and the results of research flow back to the communities they serve.

about.title: Office of the Vice President for Research and Innovation
about.description: The Office of the Vice President for Research and Innovation (OVPRI) provides strategic leadership for AUF's research enterprise — coordinating research programs, managing grants, overseeing research centers, and ensuring that the university's research outputs meet national and international standards of excellence.
roles: Formulate and implement the University's Research and Innovation Agenda
Manage and administer internal and external research grants
Oversee the operation of research centers and institutes
Coordinate research ethics review and compliance processes
Promote publication, dissemination, and utilization of research outputs
Facilitate faculty and student participation in research programs
Build partnerships with government, industry, and international research bodies

visionMission.title: Vision & Mission
visionMission.vision: To be a leading research university that generates knowledge contributing to national development and global academic discourse — guided by the Catholic values of truth, excellence, and compassionate service.
visionMission.mission: To cultivate a dynamic research culture at AUF through quality instruction in research methods, provision of research support services, management of competitive grants, and translation of research findings into policies, products, and practices that benefit communities.

centers.title: Research Centers and Institutes
centers.description: AUF's research centers serve as interdisciplinary hubs where faculty and student researchers collaborate on focused agendas that address pressing societal challenges.

items.name: Center for Data Analytics, Informatics, and Computing
items.description: The CDAIC advances applied research in data science, artificial intelligence, machine learning, and information systems — translating computational methods into practical solutions for industry, government, and society.

items.name: Center for Advanced Research and Innovation
items.description: CARI drives interdisciplinary research across engineering, health sciences, and applied natural sciences — fostering innovation that bridges the laboratory and the real world. It serves as AUF's primary hub for externally funded research and technology transfer.

items.name: Center for Applied Research in Education
items.description: CARE focuses on research in teaching, learning, and educational policy — generating evidence-based insights that improve educational practice, curriculum design, and institutional effectiveness at AUF and in partner schools and communities.

thrusts.title: Research Thrusts and Priorities
thrusts.description: AUF's Research and Innovation Agenda aligns with the national Harmonized National Research and Development Agenda (HNRDA) and the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). The following thrusts reflect the university's disciplinary strengths and its commitment to addressing the most pressing concerns of Philippine society.

items.title: Health Sciences
items.description: Research aimed at improving health outcomes, advancing medical and allied health practice, and addressing the burden of disease in Central Luzon and the Philippines.
subtopics: Public health, epidemiology, and community health
Allied health practices: nursing, pharmacy, physical therapy, medical technology

items.title: Education
items.description: Research that enhances teaching practice, learning environments, and educational policy — with special focus on basic education, higher education, and inclusive learning.

items.title: Business and Entrepreneurship
items.description: Research that advances enterprise development, economic resilience, and responsible business practice — particularly relevant to the SME landscape of Pampanga and Central Luzon.

items.title: Engineering and Technology
items.description: Applied research in engineering disciplines that addresses infrastructure, energy, manufacturing, and technological innovation in the Philippine context.

items.title: Information and Communication Technology
items.description: Research at the intersection of computing, information systems, and society — addressing digital transformation, cybersecurity, and AI-driven solutions for Philippine industries.
subtopics: Artificial intelligence and machine learning applications

items.title: Social Sciences and Humanities
items.description: Research that explores the human dimensions of society — examining culture, language, governance, and social change through rigorous social-scientific and humanistic inquiry.

items.title: Environmental and Natural Sciences
items.description: Research addressing environmental sustainability, biodiversity, climate change, and the responsible stewardship of natural resources in the Philippine context.

services.title: Research Services and Support
services.description: OVPRI provides a comprehensive suite of research support services to AUF faculty and students — from conceptualization and funding to publication and dissemination.

categories.title: Research Ethics Review
categories.description: The AUF Institutional Review Board (IRB) reviews all research involving human participants, ensuring compliance with ethical standards and the protection of research subjects.

forms.name: Adverse Event Report Form
forms.description: For reporting unanticipated issues in ongoing studies

categories.title: Internal Research Funding
categories.description: AUF provides internal research grants for faculty and student researchers through competitive application processes managed by OVPRI.

forms.name: Internal Grant Proposal Form
forms.description: Full research proposal for internal funding

forms.name: Research Fund Liquidation Form
forms.description: For reporting use of granted funds

categories.title: Publication and Dissemination Support
categories.description: OVPRI assists researchers in preparing manuscripts for publication, identifying appropriate journals, and navigating the publication process.

forms.name: Conference Travel Grant Application
forms.description: Funding for paper presentations at conferences

categories.title: Research Completion and Reporting
categories.description: All funded research must submit completion reports to OVPRI for archiving and institutional compliance.

forms.name: Research Progress Report
forms.description: Mid-term update for ongoing funded projects

forms.name: Dissemination Activity Report
forms.description: Documentation of research outputs and presentations

grants.title: Grants and Opportunities
grants.description: OVPRI actively facilitates AUF faculty and student access to external research grants from government agencies, international bodies, and private foundations.

opportunities.name: Philippine Council for Health Research and Development
opportunities.description: Competitive grants for health research with emphasis on diseases prevalent in the Philippines, medical innovations, and public health interventions.

opportunities.name: Philippine Council for Industry, Energy and Emerging Technology Research and Development
opportunities.description: Grants supporting engineering, ICT, energy, and industrial research with commercialization potential.

opportunities.name: Philippine Council for Agriculture, Aquatic and Natural Resources Research and Development
opportunities.description: Grants for environmental science, agriculture, aquatic resources, and sustainable natural resource management.

opportunities.name: CHED's Program on Maritime Research and Collaboration
opportunities.description: CHED-administered competitive grants for higher education research aligned with national development priorities.

opportunities.name: International Research Collaboration Grants
opportunities.description: OVPRI facilitates access to grants from international bodies including the British Council, Japan International Cooperation Agency (JICA), and other bilateral research programs.

applicationForms.name: External Grant Endorsement Letter Request
applicationForms.description: Required before submitting applications to funding agencies

applicationForms.name: Memorandum of Agreement Template
applicationForms.description: Standard MOA for collaborative research projects

applicationForms.name: Co-Authorship and IP Agreement
applicationForms.description: Intellectual property agreement for collaborative outputs

archive.title: Research Archive
archive.description: AUF's research archive documents the scholarly contributions of its faculty and students — from refereed journal articles and conference papers to theses and institutional publications.

featuredPublications.title: Machine Learning Approaches to Early Detection of Dengue Fever in Central Luzon
featuredPublications.authors: Santos, M.C., Reyes, J.A., and Cruz, A.L.

featuredPublications.title: Community-Based Disaster Risk Reduction in Pampanga: A Participatory Action Research Approach
featuredPublications.authors: Torres, L.M., Bautista, E.F., and Villanueva, C.J.

facultyResearch.description: AUF faculty actively engage in funded and unfunded research across all disciplines. Browse the faculty research repository for working papers, completed studies, and ongoing projects.
highlights: Browse by department, research thrust, or year
Access pre-publication manuscripts and working papers
Download theses and dissertations from the AUF Graduate School
Track citations and research impact metrics

journals.name: AUF Research Journal
journals.description: AUF's flagship peer-reviewed multidisciplinary journal publishing original research, review articles, and case studies from AUF faculty and invited contributors.

journals.name: AUF Journal of Health Sciences
journals.description: Focused publication for health sciences research outputs from the Colleges of Nursing, Allied Health Professions, and the School of Medicine.

upcomingEvents.title: AUF Research Summit 2025
upcomingEvents.description: Annual gathering of AUF researchers, faculty, and external collaborators for research presentations, workshops, and networking.

upcomingEvents.title: Research Proposal Writing Workshop
upcomingEvents.description: Workshop for AUF faculty on developing competitive research proposals for DOST, CHED, and other external funding agencies.

upcomingEvents.title: IRB Orientation for New Researchers
upcomingEvents.description: Mandatory orientation for faculty and students conducting research involving human participants.

announcements.title: Call for Research Proposals — Internal Grant Program
announcements.description: OVPRI invites AUF faculty and graduate students to submit research proposals for the University's internal grant program. Download the application forms from the Services & Support section.

announcements.title: DOST-PCHRD Grant Applications Now Open
announcements.description: The Philippine Council for Health Research and Development is accepting applications for its competitive grant program. Contact OVPRI for endorsement processing.

directory.title: Faculty Research Directory
directory.description: Explore the research profiles of AUF's faculty members — their specializations, active projects, and published works.

downloadableForms.name: Faculty Researcher Profile Form
downloadableForms.description: For registration in the AUF Faculty Research Directory

downloadableForms.name: Research Activity Update Form
downloadableForms.description: Annual update of publications, grants, and ongoing projects

downloadableForms.name: Research Load Credit Application
downloadableForms.description: Application for research load credit in lieu of teaching units
